questions.question: Why is a tangent perpendicular to the radius at the point of contact?
questions.answer: Because the radius is the shortest distance from the centre to the tangent.

questions.question: Why can only one tangent be drawn at a point on a circle?
questions.answer: Because only one line can be perpendicular to the radius at that point.

questions.question: Why does a tangent not cut a circle?
questions.answer: Because it touches the circle at only one point.

questions.question: Why is a tangent a special case of a secant?
questions.answer: Because the two points of intersection of the secant coincide.

questions.question: Why can no tangent be drawn from a point inside a circle?
questions.answer: All lines through that point intersect the circle at two points.

questions.question: Why can two tangents be drawn from a point outside a circle?
questions.answer: Because two lines from that point touch the circle at exactly one point each.

questions.question: Why are the two tangents from an external point equal?
questions.answer: Because the triangles formed are congruent by RHS.

questions.question: What is meant by the length of a tangent?
questions.answer: The distance between the external point and the point of contact.

questions.question: Why is the radius called the normal at the point of contact?
questions.answer: Because it is perpendicular to the tangent.

questions.question: Why is OP the shortest distance from centre to tangent?
questions.answer: Because the perpendicular distance is the shortest.

questions.question: What happens to a secant when its chord length becomes zero?
questions.answer: It becomes a tangent.

questions.question: Why are parallel tangents possible to a circle?
questions.answer: Because tangents can touch the circle on opposite sides.

questions.question: What geometric concept explains the equality of tangents?
questions.answer: Congruence of triangles.

questions.question: Why is the angle between radius and tangent always 90°?
questions.answer: Because the radius is perpendicular to the tangent at the point of contact.

questions.question: What is the relation between tangent and chord at contact?
questions.answer: The tangent is perpendicular to the radius drawn to the point of contact.

questions.question: Why does a tangent from an external point touch only once?
questions.answer: Because touching more than once would make it a secant.

questions.question: Why does OP bisect the angle between two tangents?
questions.answer: Because the triangles formed are congruent.

questions.question: What condition makes a line a tangent?
questions.answer: It must touch the circle at exactly one point.

questions.question: Why does every tangent have a unique point of contact?
questions.answer: Because touching at more points makes it a secant.

questions.question: Why are radii equal in tangent constructions?
questions.answer: All radii of a circle are equal.

questions.question: Why is the centre equidistant from points of contact?
questions.answer: Because both distances are radii.

questions.question: Why do tangents not pass through the interior of circle?
questions.answer: Because they only touch the boundary.

questions.question: What ensures tangent is unique at a point?
questions.answer: Perpendicularity with radius.

questions.question: Why does a chord touching inner circle get bisected?
questions.answer: Because radius drawn is perpendicular to the chord.

questions.question: Why is tangent considered linear contact?
questions.answer: Because it touches the circle at only one point.
